_usage: HowTo entity for the /how-it-works page — the canonical goal-to-governed-work lifecycle. Emit on /how-it-works only. The five HowToStep entries map to the page's five 'Step N' sections (Describe, Compile, Govern, Run, Learn) in order; Step 2's six sub-points are modelled as itemListElement HowToDirection entries under the Compile step. HowTo rich results were deprecated in Aug 2023 — do NOT promise rich results — but AI engines still parse HowTo for procedural extraction, so keep it. Each step.url is the on-page anchor; confirm the anchors match the built page's section IDs. Keep the lifecycle order intact — this is the site's canonical procedural narrative.
name: How Oraclous works — from a goal in plain language to governed work
description: How Oraclous turns a plain-language goal into governed work running across human and AI Agent Actors, in five steps: Describe, Compile, Govern, Run, and Learn — every step audited.

step.name: Describe
step.text: An Operator — the person who states the goal — writes what needs doing in plain language. There is no pipeline to wire and no code to write at this stage; the prose is the input. Oraclous separates what work needs doing (prose, written by Operators) from how the runtime enforces it (code, written once by the platform).

step.name: Compile
step.text: The Compile flow turns the goal into a reviewable Harness through a defined sequence — no black box. You review the Harness before it runs, and a prose instruction never overrides a structured policy: code wins.

itemListElement.text: Workspace survey — the compiler reads the Capabilities, Actors, data, and policies already in the workspace.
Clarifying questions — where the goal is ambiguous, it asks before committing to a plan.
Topology planning — it plans which Actors do what, in what order, and how they hand off.
Manifest emission — it emits an OHM manifest with a machine-validated structured zone and a model-interpreted prose zone.
Review dialogue — you read the proposed Harness and discuss changes.
Commit — only when you commit does it become a runnable Harness.

step.name: Govern
step.text: Every Harness runs inside a ReBAC governance envelope enforced platform-wide at the Harness level. Each Harness runs under one of five named, versioned policy sets — from development to production-strict to production-federated — each declaring trust tier, budget ceilings, signature requirements, BYOM constraints, capability allow/deny, and audit level and retention. Coded enforcement applies them; prose never overrides them.

step.name: Run
step.text: One runtime runs everything. The Harness Runtime dispatches Agents into a tool-use loop and humans into task-board assignments through the same runtime, with no privileged code path for either. Human review is built in — the runtime treats waiting on a human like waiting on a tool return. Execution is durable and checkpointed: work survives restarts, schedules fire from the Harness's triggers, runs can pause, resume, or cancel, and timeouts plus a declared retry count prevent runaway loops. Every step writes provenance and meters consumption.

step.name: Learn
step.text: As Actors do work, the Learn flow writes to Consciousness — a per-Actor record of accumulated learning the platform consults on later work (the platform's term for persistent memory, not literal sentience). Organisational memory lives in the knowledge graph: a queryable, provenance-tracked store of nodes and relationships scoped by organization_id, with ReBAC-bounded retrieval traceable to source.
